metadata.name: business-rules-enhanced
metadata.summary: Fork of Venmo-Business-Rules: Python DSL for setting up business intelligence rules that can be configured without code  History -------  1.0.1 +++++ released 2016-3-16  - Fixes a packaging bug preventing 1.0.0 from being installed on some platforms.  1.0.0 +++++ released 2016-3-16  - Removes caching layer on rule decorator

functions.name: business_rules.utils.export_rule_data
documentation:
export_rule_data is used to export all information about the
variables, actions, and operators to the client. This will return a
dictionary with three keys:
- variables: a list of all available variables along with their label, type and options
- actions: a list of all actions along with their label and params
- variable_type_operators: a dictionary of all field_types -> list of available operators

functions.name: business_rules.utils.float_to_decimal
documentation:
Convert a floating point number to a Decimal with
no loss of information. Intended for Python 2.6 where
casting float to Decimal does not work.

functions.name: business_rules.utils.get_date
documentation: Returns a utc timestamp for comparison

functions.name: business_rules.actions._validate_action_parameters
documentation:
Verifies that the parameters specified are actual parameters for the
function `func`, and that the field types are FIELD_* types in fields.

functions.name: business_rules.actions.rule_action
documentation: Decorator to make a function into a rule action

classes.name: business_rules.actions.BaseActions
documentation:
Classes that hold a collection of actions to use with the rules
engine should inherit from this.

functions.name: business_rules.six.iterlists

functions.name: business_rules.six.exec_
documentation: Execute code in a namespace.

functions.name: business_rules.six.print_

functions.name: business_rules.six.with_metaclass
documentation: Create a base class with a metaclass.

functions.name: business_rules.six.add_metaclass

classes.name: business_rules.six.X
documentation: It's possible to have sizeof(long) != sizeof(Py_ssize_t).

classes.name: business_rules.six._MovedItems
documentation: Lazy loading of moved objects

classes.name: business_rules.six.Module_six_moves_urllib_parse

classes.name: business_rules.six.Module_six_moves_urllib
documentation: Create a six.moves.urllib namespace that resembles the Python 3 namespace

functions.name: business_rules.variables.rule_variable
documentation: Decorator to make a function into a rule variable

classes.name: business_rules.variables.BaseVariables
documentation:
Classes that hold a collection of variables to use with the rules
engine should inherit from this.

functions.name: business_rules.engine.check_condition
documentation:
Checks a single rule condition - the condition will be made up of
variables, values, and the comparison operator. The defined_variables
object must have a variable defined for any variables in this condition.

functions.name: business_rules.engine._get_variable_value
documentation:
Call the function provided on the defined_variables object with the
given name (raise exception if that doesn't exist) and casts it to the
specified type.

Returns an instance of operators.BaseType

functions.name: business_rules.engine._do_operator_comparison
documentation:
Finds the method on the given operator_type and compares it to the
given comparison_value.

operator_type should be an instance of operators.BaseType
comparison_value is whatever python type to compare to
returns a bool

functions.name: business_rules.operators.export_type
documentation: Decorator to expose the given class to business_rules.export_rule_data.

functions.name: business_rules.operators.type_operator
documentation:
Decorator to make a function into a type operator.

- assert_type_for_arguments - if True this patches the operator function
  so that arguments passed to it will have _assert_valid_value_and_cast
  called on them to make type errors explicit.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType._check_equality
documentation:
Equality checks work slightly differently for clinical datasets. See truth table below:
Operator       --A         --B         Outcome
equal_to       "" or null  "" or null  False
equal_to       "" or null  Populated   False
equal_to       Populated   "" or null  False
equal_to       Populated   Populated   A == B

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType._check_inequality
documentation:
Equality checks work slightly differently for clinical datasets. See truth table below:
Operator       --A         --B         Outcome
not_equal_to   "" or null  "" or null  False
not_equal_to   "" or null  Populated   True
not_equal_to   Populated   "" or null  True
not_equal_to   Populated   Populated   A != B

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.suffix_equal_to
documentation: Checks if target suffix is equal to comparator.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.suffix_not_equal_to
documentation: Checks if target suffix is not equal to comparator.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.prefix_equal_to
documentation: Checks if target prefix is equal to comparator.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.prefix_not_equal_to
documentation: Checks if target prefix is not equal to comparator.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.prefix_is_contained_by
documentation: Checks if target prefix is contained by the comparator.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.suffix_is_contained_by
documentation: Checks if target prefix is equal to comparator.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType._check_equality_of_string_part
documentation: Checks if the given string part is equal to comparison data.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.equals_string_part
documentation:
Checks that the values in the target column
equal the result of parsing the value in the comparison
column with a regex

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.has_equal_length
documentation:
Checks that the target length is the same as comparator.
If comparing two columns (value_is_literal is False), the operator
compares lengths of values in these columns.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.longer_than
documentation:
Checks if the target is longer than the comparator.
If comparing two columns (value_is_literal is False), the operator
compares lengths of values in these columns.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.is_not_unique_relationship
documentation:
Validates one-to-one relationship between two columns (target and comparator) against a dataset.
One-to-one means that a pair of columns can be duplicated but its integrity must not be violated:
one value of target always corresponds to one value of comparator. Examples:

Valid dataset:
STUDYID  STUDYDESC
1        A
2        B
3        C
1        A
2        B

Invalid dataset:
STUDYID  STUDYDESC
1        A
2        A
3        C

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.has_next_corresponding_record
documentation:
The operator ensures that value of target in current row
is the same as value of comparator in the next row.
In order to achieve this, we just remove last row from target
and first row from comparator and compare the resulting contents.
The result is reported for target.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.compare_target_with_comparator_next_row
documentation:
Compares current row of a target with the next row of comparator.
We can't compare last row of target with the next row of comparator
because there is no row after the last one.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.present_on_multiple_rows_within
documentation:
The operator ensures that the target is present on multiple rows
within a group_by column. The dataframe is grouped by a certain column
and the check is applied to each group.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.additional_columns_empty
documentation:
The dataframe column might have some additional columns.
If the next additional column exists, the previous one cannot be empty.
Example:
    column - TSVAL
    additional columns - TSVAL1, TSVAL2, ...
    If TSVAL2 exists -> TSVAL1 cannot be empty.
    Original column (TSVAL) can be empty.

The operator extracts these additional columns from the DF
and ensures they are not empty.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.has_different_values
documentation: The operator ensures that the target column has different values.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.is_ordered_by
documentation: Checking validity based on target order.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.value_has_multiple_references
documentation:
Requires a target column and a reference count column whose values
are a dictionary containing the number of times that value appears.

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.target_is_sorted_by
documentation: Checking the sort order based on  comparators

methods.name: business_rules.operators.DataframeType.variable_metadata_equal_to
documentation:
Validates the metadata for variables, provided in the metadata column, is equal to
the comparator.
Ex.
target: STUDYID
comparator: "Exp"
metadata_column: {"STUDYID": "Req", "DOMAIN": "Req"}
result: False

classes.name: business_rules.operators.GenericType
documentation: This is meant to be a generic operator type to support all operations on a given value. Use this when you don't know the type of the value that will be returned.
